title: runtime/cgo: pthread_create failed: EAGAIN
body:
Ok, so this isn't something one would do IRL, but I was chasing a different flake and this is what happened:
```console
$ while :;do podman --storage-driver vfs pod create;podman --storage-driver vfs pod start -l; podman --storage-driver vfs stop -a -t 0;podman --storage-driver vfs pod rm -fa;podman --storage-driver vfs rm -fa;done
...works fine for an hour or two...
```

podman-3.4.0-3.fc36.x86_64, crun-1.2-1.fc36.x86_64, conmon-2.0.30-2.fc36.x86_64 on 5.14.0-0.rc5.20210813gitf8e6dfc64f61.46.fc36

Once this happens, podman is forever unusable, it always crashes the same way. Even `podman info`. I rebooted, started the reproducer again, and (an hour or two later) it crashed again.

comments.body:
see https://github.com/golang/go/issues/24484
Can you check if podman is leaking processes?

Oh nice, that looks like it. I ran `ps auxww|grep podman` and didn't see any hits other than the ~~daemon~~ namespace-keeper-whatsit; but I never ran `ps auxww|wc -l`. I wiped the VM last night; do you see a strong need for me to reproduce this again, or shall we just close as Not Podman's Problem?

Well I would assume something is leaking until you reach the max pid limit and than go starts failing.
We could also leak other processes than podman, e.g. conmon or CNI plugins. I think you should check that before we close this.
OK. It will take a few hours; if it happens during your working day, I may ping you on IRC with host login info.
OK, it crashed, just about 45 minutes. I was running `ps auxww|wc -l` in a separate window periodically, it always showed 99-102, and still does right now.
